title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Reggae, a harbor seal at the New England Aquarium, went viral for playing with a rubber duck in a video.
The video has garnered nearly 500,000 views and highlights Reggae's intelligence and curiosity, according to aquarium staff.
Aquarium trainers engage seals with enrichment activities like 'Find It' to simulate natural behaviors and keep them stimulated.

title: What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres on Bravo April 2 at 9 p.m.
The show features a cast including a former TV anchor, a pizzeria co-owner, and a cannabis dispensary operator.
The Red Sox will host a Bravo Night with the show's cast at Fenway Park on April 17.

title: 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 24 Boston restaurants received approval for new or upgraded liquor licenses, enhancing their ability to serve alcoholic beverages.
13 businesses gained licenses via state legislation granting Boston 225 new licenses, benefiting areas like East Boston and Roslindale.
Six restaurants successfully upgraded beer and wine licenses to all-alcohol through recent local legislation initiatives.

title: Mass. man nabbed after allegedly driving over 100 mph in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 21-year-old Attleboro man was arrested for driving 104 mph in a 55 mph zone on Route 202 in Rindge, NH.
The driver of a 2020 Kia Stinger was charged with reckless operation after being stopped by Rindge Police at 11 p.m.
He faces at least a $750 fine and 90-day license suspension if convicted, with arraignment scheduled for April 5.

title: AirTag leads volunteers to hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Volunteers from The Cat Connection used an AirTag to track and locate a stray cat and her six kittens.
The cat, named Emerald, led rescuers to her kittens inside a self-storage facility in Waltham.
The cat family has been moved to a foster home and will be available for adoption in about two months.

title: N.H. Senate approves toll hike for out-of-state drivers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The N.H. Senate approved increased tolls for out-of-state drivers to fund transportation projects, despite Gov. Kelly Ayotte's opposition.
Proposed toll hikes aim to raise up to $81.4 million by 2036 for infrastructure improvements not covered by the current 10-year plan.
Out-of-state drivers face toll increases of $0.50 to $1 at various N.H. locations; N.H. E-ZPass users are exempt.

title: One dead after crash in Tewksbury
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: One person died after a head-on collision between two vehicles on Andover Street in Tewksbury, Sunday evening.
Both drivers were treated at the scene and transported to local hospitals, where one later succumbed to their injuries.
Andover Street was closed for several hours for a crash reconstruction investigation; police have not released the deceased's identity.

title: Patriots reportedly cut linebacker Jahlani Tavai
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots released linebacker Jahlani Tavai after five seasons, reports indicate.
Tavai recorded over 110 tackles in 2023 and 2024 but played only 12 games in 2025.
He was diagnosed with a rare autoimmune disease and faced personal tragedy before the 2025 season.

title: RFK Jr. blasted Dunkin’ for selling sugary drinks. Which menu items fit his criteria?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. criticized Dunkin' for selling drinks with high sugar content, sparking discussions on safety data.
Large Dunkin’ drinks like Butter Pecan Swirl and Caramel Creme Frozen Coffee contain up to 172 grams of sugar, nearly meeting Kennedy's concerns.
A total of 40 Dunkin' beverages exceed 115 grams of sugar, highlighting the contrast with recommended daily sugar intake guidelines.

title: Two Mass. lottery players win big on new scratch ticket games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Fernandes of Woburn claimed a $4 million prize in the 'Cash Doubler' game, taking home $2.6 million after taxes.
Yancy Contreras Menjivar of Nantucket won a $2 million prize in the 'Stacked' game, receiving $1.3 million after taxes.
Old South Diner in Nantucket, which sold Menjivar's winning ticket, will receive a $20,000 bonus for the sale.
